edge_hypothesis: EMA crossover has been dismissed as 'noise' (PF=1.03) — but ONLY at low R:R (2:1). The system's foundational discovery is that extreme R:R amplifies hidden edge: MACD went from PF=1.03 at 2:1 to PF=1.712 at 7:1. EMA_9/EMA_21 crossover is the SIMPLEST possible trend signal — two moving averages crossing. This spec tests whether extreme R:R (7:1 through 10:1) can extract tail-harvesting edge from this simple signal. THREE POSSIBLE OUTCOMES: (1) If PF > 1.2 at 7:1+, then signal complexity doesn't matter — any trend detection works for tail harvesting, and we should add ema_crossover to the portfolio as a high-frequency, low-correlation diversifier. (2) If PF = 1.0-1.1, the signal captures SOME directional edge but not enough to overcome fees at extreme R:R — intermediate signal quality. (3) If PF < 1.0, ema_crossover's signal quality is fundamentally insufficient for tail harvesting, confirming that MACD's histogram zero-line cross provides materially better signal quality than a simple EMA cross. This is a DIAGNOSTIC experiment — it validates or refutes the thesis that 'any trend signal works with extreme R:R.' Regime assumption: ALL (ema_crossover takes signals in all regimes to maximize trade count for statistical significance). Invalidation: If PF < 0.9 at 7:1 R:R with 200+ trades, signal quality is the bottleneck, not R:R — close the ema_crossover chapter permanently.

variants.name: ema_crossover_1h_tail_test_7to1
variants.description: EMA_9/EMA_21 crossover on ETH/1h with 7:1 R:R (1.5x ATR stop / 10.5x ATR TP). Direct comparison to MACD 1h 7:1 (the portability test from mc7h1f6p). EMA crossover generates more signals than MACD (every EMA cross vs. histogram zero-line cross), so expect 250-400 trades. Higher trade count means better statistical significance. If PF > 1.2 with 300+ trades, ema_crossover becomes a viable portfolio component due to signal frequency and simplicity. Breakeven win rate: 12.5% after fees.
entry_long: EMA_9 crosses above EMA_21 (fast EMA bullish crossover).
entry_short: EMA_9 crosses below EMA_21 (fast EMA bearish crossover).
filters: Backtester executable indicators: EMA, SMA, RSI, ATR, MACD, Bollinger Bands, Stochastic, ADX, CCI, Williams %R, OBV, VWAP, Ichimoku, Supertrend, Donchian Channels.
No additional trend filter — testing raw signal quality
exit_rules: Exit on ATR-based take profit hit (10.5x ATR).
Exit on ATR-based stop loss hit (1.5x ATR).
R:R = 7:1. Needs 12.5% win rate to break even after fees.
Diagnostic: does the simplest trend signal produce tail-harvesting PF > 1.1?
risk_rules: Stop loss at 1.5x ATR_14 below/above entry.
Take profit at 10.5x ATR_14 from entry.

components.notes: EMA_9 and EMA_21 crossover — simplest trend signal in the system
EMA crossover IS the signal — no secondary confirmation, testing raw signal quality
ATR_14 for 1.5x stop / 10.5x TP — STOP_FLOOR compliant, 7:1 R:R

variants.name: ema_crossover_4h_tail_test_8to1
variants.description: EMA_9/EMA_21 crossover on ETH/4h with 8:1 R:R (1.5x ATR stop / 12.0x ATR TP). 4h timeframe reduces noise compared to 1h — fewer whipsaw crosses, higher signal quality per cross. On 4h, EMA crosses should produce 60-120 trades (vs. 250-400 on 1h). If 4h PF > 1h PF, it confirms that EMA crossover's weakness is noise-frequency, not signal logic. 8:1 R:R selected because MACD's 4h peak is at 7:1 — testing whether EMA crossover's simpler signal benefits from slightly higher R:R to compensate for lower signal quality. Breakeven win rate: 11.1%.
entry_long: EMA_9 crosses above EMA_21 (fast EMA bullish crossover).
entry_short: EMA_9 crosses below EMA_21 (fast EMA bearish crossover).
filters: Backtester executable indicators: EMA, SMA, RSI, ATR, MACD, Bollinger Bands, Stochastic, ADX, CCI, Williams %R, OBV, VWAP, Ichimoku, Supertrend, Donchian Channels.
No additional trend filter — testing raw signal quality on 4h
exit_rules: Exit on ATR-based take profit hit (12.0x ATR).
Exit on ATR-based stop loss hit (1.5x ATR).
R:R = 8:1. Needs 11.1% win rate to break even after fees.
4h noise reduction test: does fewer crosses = higher PF per cross?
risk_rules: Stop loss at 1.5x ATR_14 below/above entry.
Take profit at 12.0x ATR_14 from entry.

components.notes: EMA_9/EMA_21 crossover on 4h — reduced noise vs 1h
EMA crossover signal only — no MACD/RSI filter, pure trend detection
ATR_14 for 1.5x stop / 12.0x TP — 8:1 R:R, STOP_FLOOR compliant

variants.name: ema_crossover_1h_extreme_rr_10to1
variants.description: EMA_9/EMA_21 crossover on ETH/1h with 10:1 R:R (1.5x ATR stop / 15.0x ATR TP). The extreme end of the R:R curve for the simplest signal. MACD at 12:1 on 4h still produces PF=1.302 — does ema_crossover have ANY tail-harvesting capability at this extreme? 10:1 means breakeven at 9.1% win rate. With 300+ expected trades, even a 10% win rate produces PF > 1.0. If this variant works (PF > 1.1), ema_crossover captures genuine trend continuation tails despite its simplicity. This is the stress test variant — if it fails while the 7:1 works, we know ema_crossover's R:R ceiling. If BOTH fail, ema_crossover is confirmed noise regardless of R:R.
entry_long: EMA_9 crosses above EMA_21 (fast EMA bullish crossover).
entry_short: EMA_9 crosses below EMA_21 (fast EMA bearish crossover).
filters: Backtester executable indicators: EMA, SMA, RSI, ATR, MACD, Bollinger Bands, Stochastic, ADX, CCI, Williams %R, OBV, VWAP, Ichimoku, Supertrend, Donchian Channels.
Extreme R:R stress test — 10:1 on simplest signal
exit_rules: Exit on ATR-based take profit hit (15.0x ATR).
Exit on ATR-based stop loss hit (1.5x ATR).
R:R = 10:1. Needs 9.1% win rate to break even after fees.

risk_rules: Stop loss at 1.5x ATR_14 below/above entry.
Take profit at 15.0x ATR_14 from entry.
